front: Short Story: an Alabama farmer is tricked by a disguised Federal scout with driftwood; hallucinates escaping while being hanged; receives a 'stunning blow' to his neck
back: An Occurrence at Owl Creek Bridge (Ambrose Bierce)
work: An Occurrence at Owl Creek Bridge

front: Short Story: the protagonist is disturbed by the sound of his own watch 'as slow as the tolling of a death knell'
back: An Occurrence at Owl Creek Bridge (Ambrose Bierce)
work: An Occurrence at Owl Creek Bridge

front: Short Story: a chess-playing robot murders its inventor; the narrator is haunted by 'Consciousness is the creature of Rhythm'
Short Story: the inventor uses eucalyptus roots seeking water to prove plants think; narrator saved from fire by metal worker Haley
Short Story: a deaf-mute child with a wooden sword encounters retreating Civil War soldiers and discovers his family's burning home
Work: satirical dictionary defining education as something that 'disguises from the foolish their lack of understanding'; also called 'The Cynic's Wordbook'
Short Story: the title character writes an incomplete poem ending 'At last the viewless...' in blood; attacked by the zombified spirit of his mother Catherine Larue
back: The Death of Halpin Frayser (Ambrose Bierce)

front: Short Story: the title character worships Hastur and pursues a frequently-disappearing maiden named 'Happiness'; the deity was later adopted by Lovecraft
Author: fictionalized by Carlos Fuentes as the 'Indiana General' carrying Don Quixote and a Colt .44 during the Mexican Revolution
